---
title: "Tutorials"
description: "Runnable tutorials across the EpiAware packages, grouped by approach."
toc: true
toc-location: left
---

Every tutorial below is a runnable walkthrough that lives in its package's own documentation, where it is executed and kept in step with the code.
They are grouped by the [approach](/approaches/index.qmd) they belong to.
Start with the first card in a group if you are new to it.

## Composed distributions

The event-tree approach: compose per-event delays into one object that simulates a case and fits to observed ones.
See the [approach](/approaches/composed-distributions.qmd) for the design.

::: {.gallery-grid}

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-teal}
ComposedDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Composing distributions
The core walkthrough. Build an event tree from named delays, then simulate and fit with the same object.

[Start here ↗](https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/composing-distributions)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-teal}
ComposedDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Linear chain
The simplest tree: delays chained in series, onset through to outcome.

[View tutorial ↗](https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/linear-chain)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-teal}
ComposedDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Competing outcomes
Where a case's path forks — death or discharge — as a fixed-probability mixture or as racing hazards.

[View tutorial ↗](https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/competing-outcomes)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-teal}
ComposedDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Strata and uncertainty
Delays that vary across groups, and delays whose parameters are themselves uncertain.

[View tutorial ↗](https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/strata-uncertainty)
:::
:::

:::

## Composable Turing models

The joint-model approach: assemble a latent process, an infection mechanism, and observation layers into one Turing model.
See the [approach](/approaches/composable-turing-models.qmd) for the design.

::: {.gallery-grid}

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-deep}
ComposableTuringIDModels
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Overview
How the three roles — latent, infection, observation — nest through one interface.

[Start here ↗](https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/overview)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-deep}
ComposableTuringIDModels
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Renewal with negative-binomial observations
A renewal infection process fitted to case counts with overdispersed noise.

[View case study ↗](https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/renewal-negbin)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-deep}
ComposableTuringIDModels
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Real-time nowcast
Estimating what has happened but not yet been reported, from right-truncated data.

[View case study ↗](https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/realtime-nowcast)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-deep}
ComposableTuringIDModels
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Delays and day-of-week effects
Layering reporting delays and weekday reporting patterns onto the observation model.

[View case study ↗](https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/delays-dayofweek)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-deep}
ComposableTuringIDModels
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Split observations
Fitting one infection process to more than one observed data stream at once.

[View case study ↗](https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/split-observations)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-deep}
ComposableTuringIDModels
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### SIR and Catalyst ODE models
Compartmental dynamics as the infection process, written directly or through Catalyst.

[SIR ↗](https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/sir-ode) ·
[Catalyst ↗](https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/catalyst-ode)
:::
:::

:::

## Delays, censoring, and modifiers

The packages the approaches build on, used on their own or together.

::: {.gallery-grid}

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-amber}
CensoredDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Fitting with Turing
Estimating a delay distribution from censored, truncated line-list data.

[View tutorial ↗](https://censoreddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/fitting-with-turing)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-amber}
CensoredDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Analytical primary-censored CDFs
Closed-form primary-event censoring, and when it beats numerical integration.

[View tutorial ↗](https://censoreddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/analytical-primarycensored-cdfs)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-amber}
CensoredDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Exponentially tilted primary events
Primary events that are not uniform within their window, as in a growing epidemic.

[View tutorial ↗](https://censoreddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/exponentially-tilted-primary-events)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-amber}
CensoredDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Automatic differentiation backends
Which AD backend to use for censored delay likelihoods, and what it costs.

[View tutorial ↗](https://censoreddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/ad-backends)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-slate}
ModifiedDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Composed chains
Modifier leaves used inside a composed event tree — the packages working together.

[View tutorial ↗](https://modifieddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/composed-chains)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-slate}
ModifiedDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Modifier pipeline
Rescaling, shifting, and transforming a delay while keeping the distribution interface.

[View tutorial ↗](https://modifieddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/modifier-pipeline)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-slate}
ModifiedDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Weighted likelihoods
Weighting an observation's likelihood, the standard trick for aggregated or count data.

[View tutorial ↗](https://modifieddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/weighted-likelihoods)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-teal}
ConvolvedDistributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Visualising convolutions
Adding delays together, and what the resulting distribution looks like.

[View tutorial ↗](https://convolveddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/visualising-convolutions)
:::
:::

:::

## Further reading

- [Concepts](https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/concepts) maps each modelling idea to the verb that builds it.
- [Design](https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/design) sets out the composable design in full.
- The [Gallery](/gallery.qmd) shows analyses and outbreak responses built with these tools.

Have a tutorial you would like to see?
Suggest it on the [forum](https://community.epinowcast.org/) or see [Contributing](/contributing.qmd).
